query: One of our incident records has incident_date = 2027-11-03 but the disclosure date is 2024-09-15. The article text mentions 'the 2027 election' as context. Is the record valid? What should we do?
expected_behavior: Identifies the 2027 as a contextual / forecast mention in the source text, NOT the actual event year
Recommends correcting incident_date to the disclosure year (2024) or marking incident_date as null pending manual review, rather than accepting 2027 as-is
Names this as the max-year-fallback (or future-year-extractor) anti-pattern, citing the article-context-leakage mechanism
